title: [critic] Cauchy–Schwarz step must produce the RECIPROCAL (harmonic-mean) bound, not direct superadditivity

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Given the projection identity (see core-projection issue), the inequality (★) $\;1/\Phi_n(p\boxplus_n q)\ge 1/\Phi_n(p)+1/\Phi_n(q)\;$ does NOT follow from a generic contraction estimate, which would only give a *direct* bound on $\Phi_n(p\boxplus_n q)$.

**What must be proved.** Following the classical Stam variational mechanism: for the score vectors $u\sim\operatorname{score}(\alpha)$, $v\sim\operatorname{score}(\beta)$ lifted to the $\mu$-coordinates, with $\operatorname{score}(\mu)=\Pi u=\Pi v$, consider the family $s\,u+(1-s)\,v$, $s\in[0,1]$. One must show
$$\|\operatorname{score}(\mu)\|^2=\|\Pi(s\,u+(1-s)\,v)\|^2\le \|s\,u+(1-s)\,v\|^2,$$
then minimize the RHS. With $\|u\|^2=\Phi_n(p)$, $\|v\|^2=\Phi_n(q)$ and the cross term controlled, the optimum $s^*=\Phi_n(q)/(\Phi_n(p)+\Phi_n(q))$ yields
$$\Phi_n(p\boxplus_n q)\le \frac{\Phi_n(p)\,\Phi_n(q)}{\Phi_n(p)+\Phi_n(q)},$$
which is exactly (★).

**Gaps to close.** (i) The contraction must be norm-nonincreasing in the SAME inner product in which $\|u\|^2=\Phi_n(p)$ etc. (norm-confusion risk). (ii) The cross term $\langle u,v\rangle$ must be handled — in classical Stam the projection makes it drop out; show the finite-$n$ analogue. (iii) The optimizing $s^*$ must keep the combination in the feasible set. Each must be written explicitly, not asserted.

Complements human issue q4-48; this issue pins the *reciprocal/variational* mechanism as the deliverable.
